title: The Driftwood Court
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A maritime lawyer reopens a shipwreck case on an island courthouse built from salvage.
long_description:
The Driftwood Court follows Mara Venn, an adult maritime lawyer, through Gull Court island as a maritime lawyer reopens a shipwreck case on an island courthouse built from salvage. The inquiry moves through offices, sealed rooms, public hearings, and physical evidence, keeping its suspense grounded in work, speech, and consequence.

As Owen Pike, Sel Calder, and Judge Honora Vale help test the record, Jonas Quill tries to turn delay into truth. The novel is clean adult suspense about institutions, memory, and the cost of naming what everyone was paid not to see.
